tool_name: Dust Explosion Severity — Kst Classification, Pmax & Vent Area per EN 14491 / NFPA 68

inputs.name: Dust Explosion Constant (K_st)
inputs.note: From ISO 6184-1 20-L sphere test; St1: 1–200; St2: 201–300; St3: >300 bar·m/s

inputs.name: Maximum Explosion Pressure (P_max)
inputs.note: From 20-L sphere test; most organic dusts: P_max = 7–10 bar(g)
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Transducer accuracy class 1.0: ±1% of span

inputs.name: Maximum Reduced Explosion Pressure (P_red)
inputs.note: Structural strength of enclosure; thin sheet metal ≈ 0.05–0.1 bar(g); reinforced ≈ 0.5–2.0 bar(g)
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Transducer accuracy class 1.0: ±1% of span

inputs.name: Vent Closure Opening Pressure (P_stat)
inputs.note: Bursting disc or vent panel opening pressure; must be < P_red; typical 0.02–0.1 bar(g)
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Transducer accuracy class 1.0: ±1% of span

inputs.name: Enclosure Length-to-Diameter Ratio (L/D)
inputs.note: Silos/bins with L/D > 5: turbulence correction required; EN 14491 §5.2.3

inputs.name: Dust Explosion Class
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

formulas: A_v_m2 = Math.pow(10, (0.569 * Math.log10(Kst_bar_m_s) - 0.5 * Math.log10(Pred_bar + 1) + 0.4 * Math.log10(vessel_volume_m3) - 0.595))   // [m²] Vent Area — EN 14491 Nomograph Equation (A_v) Reference: EN 14491:2012 Eq.(1) — vent area from K_st, P_red, V nomograph; valid for P_stat ≤ 0.1 bar(g)
C_stat = (Pstat_bar > 0.1) ? 1 + 0.6 * Pstat_bar : 1.0   // [dimensionless] P_stat Correction Factor (C_stat) Reference: EN 14491 §5.2.2 — P_stat correction applied when P_stat > 0.1 bar(g)

C_LD = (L_D_ratio <= 2) ? 1.0 : (L_D_ratio <= 5) ? 1 + 0.05 * (L_D_ratio - 2) : 1.15 + 0.10 * (L_D_ratio - 5)   // [dimensionless] L/D Elongated Vessel Correction Factor (C_LD) Reference: EN 14491 §5.2.3 — elongation factor for L/D > 2; St3 dusts in elongated vessels require additional analysis
A_v_design_m2 = A_v_m2 * C_stat * C_LD   // [m²] Final Required Vent Area (A_v_design) Reference: EN 14491 §5.2 — final design area including all correction factors

A_nfpa_m2 = Math.exp(-1.958 + 0.8759 * Math.log(vessel_volume_m3) + 0.5765 * Math.log(Kst_bar_m_s) - 1.3985 * Math.log(Pred_bar + 1))   // [m²] NFPA 68 Vent Area Cross-Check (Low-Strength Enclosures) Reference: NFPA 68-2018 Eq.(6.9) — alternate vent area for cross-verification; EN and NFPA should agree within 20%

Av_V_ratio = A_v_design_m2 / vessel_volume_m3; governing = (A_v_design_m2 >= A_nfpa_m2) ? 'EN 14491 governs' : 'NFPA 68 governs'   // [m²/m³, text] Vent-to-Volume Ratio & Governing Standard Reference: Conservative: use larger of EN and NFPA result; typical ratio 0.005–0.1 m²/m³

V1.error_msg: P_stat ≥ P_red: vent will not open before structural failure — P_stat must be ≤ 0.5 × P_red per EN 14491 §4.2

V2.error_msg: P_red ≥ P_max: structurally the enclosure withstands full explosion — venting not required for protection; verify intent

V3.error_msg: St3 dust in elongated vessel: EN 14491 nomograph validity limit exceeded — perform CFD or consult VDI 2263 Part 7 for St3 + L/D > 5 combinations

smart_warnings.message: St3 dust: highly energetic explosion — venting alone may be insufficient; evaluate suppression system, inherently safe design, or inerting as primary protection per NFPA 69
Vent area > 50% of vessel volume (proxy): very large vent relative to enclosure — verify structural integrity of remaining panels; consider suppression as alternative
EN and NFPA results differ by > 30%: at boundary conditions or outside nomograph validity — seek qualified dust explosion specialist review
Very low P_red on large volume: extremely large vent area required — light-duty enclosures (P_red < 0.07 bar) unsuitable for large dust collectors; reinforce or use suppression
High-end St1 approaching St2 boundary: ensure test conditions (concentration, moisture, particle size) represent worst-case — conservative K_st selection critical for vent sizing

fmea.effect: P_stat ≥ P_red: vent will not open before structural failure — P_stat must be ≤ 0.5 × P_red per EN 14491 §4.2
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: P_red ≥ P_max: structurally the enclosure withstands full explosion — venting not required for protection; verify intent
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: St3 dust in elongated vessel: EN 14491 nomograph validity limit exceeded — perform CFD or consult VDI 2263 Part 7 for St3 + L/D > 5 combinations
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: St3 dust: highly energetic explosion — venting alone may be insufficient; evaluate suppression system, inherently safe design, or inerting as primary protection per NFPA 69
Vent area > 50% of vessel volume (proxy): very large vent relative to enclosure — verify structural integrity of remaining panels; consider suppression as alternative
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: A_v_design_m2 > vessel_volume_m3 * 0.5

fmea.failureMode: EN and NFPA results differ by > 30%: at boundary conditions or outside nomograph validity — seek qualified dust explosion specialist review
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Math.abs(A_v_design_m2 - A_nfpa_m2) / A_v_design_m2 > 0.3

fmea.failureMode: Very low P_red on large volume: extremely large vent area required — light-duty enclosures (P_red < 0.07 bar) unsuitable for large dust collectors; reinforce or use suppression
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Pred_bar < 0.07 && vessel_volume_m3 > 100

fmea.failureMode: High-end St1 approaching St2 boundary: ensure test conditions (concentration, moisture, particle size) represent worst-case — conservative K_st selection critical for vent sizing
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: dust_class === 'St1' && Kst_bar_m_s > 150
